title: Vaccine safety: Resources for health professionals on surveillance, reporting and regulation - Canada.ca
content:
## Vaccine safety: Resources for health professionals on surveillance, reporting and regulation
- Adverse events following immunization (AEFI)
- Canadian Immunization Guide
- Causality assessments
- Vaccine safety surveillance and reporting
The safety of vaccines is carefully monitored, starting early in product development and continuing for as long as a vaccine is used. The following resources detail:
- what is done before and after vaccines are approved for use
- what is known about the benefits and safety of specific vaccines

## Adverse events following immunization (AEFI)
- Reported side effects following COVID-19 vaccination in Canada

## Canadian Immunization Guide
- Chapter on vaccine safety

## Causality assessments
- Advisory Committee on Causality Assessment Determines the likelihood that an adverse event is caused by vaccination
- Determines the likelihood that an adverse event is caused by vaccination
- Regulating vaccines for human use in Canada

## Vaccine safety surveillance and reporting
- Canadian Adverse Event Following Immunization Surveillance System (CAEFISS)
- Vaccine safety and possible side effects
- Immunization resources for health professionals: Printable awareness products
- Vaccines and immunization: Publications and reports
